title: caltha rotundifolia var. howellii
intro: Caltha rotundifolia var. howellii, also known as the marsh marigold, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce swelling and pain. The health benefits of Caltha rotundifolia var. howellii include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
